map: EXECUTION LAYER. C:/Users/persi/Desktop/MCPenny/makecrazypenny/execution/hyperliquid.py — sync testnet client `HyperliquidPaperClient(settings: Settings|None)` wrapping hyperliquid-python-sdk (optional 'trade' extra). Module constants: _MIN_ORDER_USD=10.0, _PRICE_SIG_FIGS=5, _MAX_PRICE_DECIMALS=6. `_build_clients(settings: Settings) -> tuple[Info, Exchange, address:str]` (line 119) is THE monkeypatch seam: lazily imports eth_account.Account + hyperliquid.exchange.Exchange + hyperliquid.info.Info, builds Account.from_key(settings.hl_private_key), address = settings.hl_account_address or account.address, base_url = settings.hyperliquid_testnet_url (the ONLY base URL — no mainnet field exists on Settings; asserted by tests/test_execution.py::test_testnet_url_is_the_only_base). Raises ExecutionError (message pre-scrubbed via core/redact.redact_secrets in ExecutionError.__init__) on missing SDK (install hint), unset key, or malformed key. Client lazy-wires via `_ensure()`; caches info.meta() in self._meta. Helpers: `_round_size` (round to coin szDecimals), `_round_price` (5 sig figs then <= 6-szDecimals decimals), `_mid_price` (info.all_mids), `_side_to_is_buy` (LONG/BUY/B/BID->True, SHORT/SELL/A/ASK->False), `_normalize_order_result` (flattens SDK response to {status, statuses?, error?}). READS: `account_state()` -> {network:'testnet', address, account_value (marginSummary.accountValue), spot_usdc (info.spot_user_state USDC total; HL keeps spot/perp collateral separate), tradable_usdc = account_value+spot_usdc, total_margin_used, total_notional_position, withdrawable, positions:[{coin, size (szi, signed), entry_price, position_value, unrealized_pnl, leverage, leverage_type, liquidation_price, margin_used}], disclaimer}. `open_orders()` -> prefers info.frontend_open_orders (falls back to info.open_orders); rows {coin, oid, side BUY/SELL, size, limit_price, timestamp, order_type?, reduce_only?, is_trigger?/trigger_price?/trigger_condition?}. `recent_fills(limit=20)` -> info.user_fills, newest first, rows {coin, side, price, size, closed_pnl (per-fill realized PnL from HL closedPnl), fee, oid, time}. WRITES: `set_leverage(symbol, leverage, cross=True)` caps to coin maxLeverage, exchange.update_leverage. `open_position(symbol, side, *, size=None, notional_usd=None, leverage=None, order_type='market', limit_price=None, reduce_only=False, slippage=None, stop_loss=None, take_profit=None)`: coin via core/symbols.to_hyperliquid_coin (BTCUSDT->BTC); sets leverage first if given; ref price = limit_price (limit) else live mid; sz = size or notional_usd/ref_px, rounded to szDecimals (reject if rounds to 0 or notional < $10); market -> exchange.market_open(coin,is_buy,sz,None,slip) with slip=settings.hl_default_slippage default 0.05; limit -> exchange.order(coin,is_buy,sz,px,{'limit':{'tif':'Gtc'}},reduce_only). If stop_loss/take_profit given: only attaches TP/SL when result status=='ok' AND a statuses[].filled.totalSz exists — sized to the actual fill, side flipped (close side = not entry side); resting limit entry returns tpsl:{skipped:...}; a failed attach never voids the entry (tpsl:{error:...}). `set_position_tpsl(symbol, *, stop_loss, take_profit, size=None, slippage=None)`: reads live position to derive close side (szi<0 -> closing buy), size defaults to full |position|; delegates to `_place_tpsl` which validates each trigger vs live mid (SL/TP on wrong side -> ExecutionError 'would trigger immediately'), builds reduce-only trigger-MARKET requests {order_type:{trigger:{triggerPx, isMarket:True, tpsl:'sl'|'tp'}}, reduce_only:True, limit_px = trigger*(1±slip) slippage bound}, and submits via exchange.bulk_orders(requests, grouping='positionTpsl') — the positionTpsl grouping is what makes the exchange treat them as an OCO pair tied to the position (one leg executing or the position closing cancels the other; survives client disconnect). `close_position(symbol, size=None, slippage=None)` -> exchange.market_close. `cancel_order(symbol, oid)` -> exchange.cancel. ORCHESTRATION. C:/Users/persi/Desktop/MCPenny/makecrazypenny/orchestration/paper_trade.py — async wrappers; client is sync so every call goes through `_run(fn,...)` = asyncio.to_thread + catch-all converting ExecutionError/any Exception to {'error': redacted, 'disclaimer'} (never raises). Manual primitives: available_pairs (keyless, core/crypto_universe.fetch_hyperliquid_perps), account, open_orders, recent_fills, set_leverage, open_manual, set_tpsl, close, cancel — all accept `client=` injection (test seam #2) and `settings=`. `open_from_decision(symbol, *, interval='15m', leverage_cap=20.0, notional_usd=None, attach_tpsl=True, settings=None, client=None)`: runs orchestration.crypto.decide_crypto, reads d['leverage'] plan (produced by analysis/leverage.py: keys suggested_leverage, max_safe_leverage, liquidation_price, stop_price, target_price, stop_distance_pct, notional_pct, margin_pct, risk_per_trade_pct, est_funding_cost); direction not LONG/SHORT -> returns {placed:False, reason, decision} with NO order; reads account_state first, equity = tradable_usdc (falls back to account_value); notional = notional_usd override OR equity * plan['notional_pct'] (zero equity / zero notional -> placed:False); leverage = plan['suggested_leverage']; with attach_tpsl, stop_loss=plan['stop_price'], take_profit=plan['target_price'] ride along into open_position (market order); returns {symbol, interval, decision, plan, placed, sizing:{equity, notional_usd, leverage, side, stop_loss, take_profit}, order, account_after (re-read on success), disclaimer}. CONFIG. C:/Users/persi/Desktop/MCPenny/makecrazypenny/core/config.py — dataclass `Settings` + `Settings.from_env()` (optional python-dotenv load, override=False; never raises; no network). Execution fields: hyperliquid_testnet_url (MCP_HL_TESTNET_URL, default https://api.hyperliquid-testnet.xyz), hl_private_key (MCP_HL_PRIVATE_KEY), hl_account_address (MCP_HL_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS), hl_default_slippage (MCP_HL_SLIPPAGE, 0.05). Crypto risk-policy fields consumed by the decision engine that feeds open_from_decision: crypto_max_leverage (MCP_CRYPTO_MAX_LEVERAGE, 20), crypto_risk_per_trade (MCP_CRYPTO_RISK_PER_TRADE, 0.025), crypto_maint_margin_rate (MCP_CRYPTO_MAINT_MARGIN, 0.005), crypto_target_vol (MCP_CRYPTO_TARGET_VOL, 0.80), crypto_liq_buffer (MCP_CRYPTO_LIQ_BUFFER, 0.5). Other env: MCP_CACHE_DIR (cache_dir, default %TEMP%/.mcpenny_cache), MCP_CAPABILITY_CHAINS (JSON) + MCP_CHAIN_<CAPABILITY> (CSV) chain overrides, MCP_BINANCE_BASE_URL, MCP_BYBIT_BASE_URL, MCP_EDGAR_USER_AGENT, MCP_MAX_DEPTH(3), MCP_MAX_BUDGET_USD(1.0), MCP_DEBATE_ROUNDS(2), MCP_CIRCUIT_FAIL_THRESHOLD(5), MCP_CIRCUIT_COOLDOWN_S(60), MCP_L2_CACHE('0'/'false' disables), provider keys ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY/FINNHUB_API_KEY/FMP_API_KEY/MARKETAUX_API_KEY/COINGECKO_API_KEY. `get_api_key(env_var)`: known names (incl. MCP_HL_PRIVATE_KEY) resolve ONLY from the injected Settings field — explicit None means disabled, no ambient-env fallback (test isolation); unknown names fall back to os.environ. REDACTION. core/redact.py `redact_secrets(text) -> str`: masks secret query params (?token=/&apikey=/key/secret/access_token), NAME=value assignments matching MCP_HL_PRIVATE_KEY|private[_-]?key|secret[_-]?key, and any standalone 64-hex (±0x) Ethereum private key (40-hex addresses deliberately untouched); idempotent; ExecutionError and _run route every error string through it. TESTS. tests/test_execution.py: fixture monkeypatches `hx._build_clients` with `lambda settings: (FakeInfo(), FakeExchange(), _ADDRESS)`; FakeInfo serves meta/all_mids/user_state/spot_user_state/open_orders/user_fills; FakeExchange records calls and returns canned ok responses (update_leverage, market_open, order, market_close, bulk_orders, cancel — signatures mirror the SDK incl. cloid=None params). Covers coin mapping, szDecimals rounding, $10 min, side mapping, leverage cap, OCO positionTpsl grouping + slippage-bounded limit_px (sl 58000 -> limit 55100 @ 5%), wrong-side trigger rejection, fill-sized TP/SL attach, resting-limit skip, entry-kept-on-bad-tpsl, testnet lock, missing-key/missing-SDK errors (real _build_clients, guarded by importlib.util.find_spec). tests/test_paper_trade.py: monkeypatches pt.decide_crypto with a FakeDecision and injects FakeClient via the `client=` kwarg — covers equity*notional_pct sizing (1000*0.5=500), notional override, spot-collateral sizing (tradable_usdc when perp equity 0), AVOID->no order, zero-equity block, error->dict, plan TP/SL pass-through, attach_tpsl=False naked order, available_pairs delegation. MCP surface: makecrazypenny/mcp_server.py lines 593-771 register paper_pairs, paper_account, paper_orders (combines open_orders + recent_fills), paper_trade_decision, paper_open, paper_set_tpsl, paper_close, paper_cancel, paper_set_leverage. ANSWERS: (4) PnL: unrealized per OPEN position — yes (positions[].unrealized_pnl + entry_price/size/margin_used); realized — per FILL via recent_fills closed_pnl + fee, so per-coin realized PnL is computable by summing fills, BUT per-trade attribution requires reconstructing entry/exit episodes from raw fills (oid/time) yourself: no client order id (cloid never passed to exchange.order/market_open although the SDK supports it), no time-window pagination (info.user_fills_by_time not wrapped; default limit=20, HL caps user_fills at most-recent ~2000), funding payments not read at all (no user_funding_history wrapper), and the decision->order-oid link exists only transiently inside the returned dict (result.statuses[].filled/resting.oid). (5) Persistent journal: NONE for decisions or trades. Nothing in execution/ or orchestration/paper_trade.py writes to disk; open_from_decision's rich {decision, plan, sizing, order, account_after} record is returned and discarded. Only disk artifacts in the codebase: provider L2 cache (providers/cache.py under Settings.cache_dir), universe snapshots (core/universe.py, core/crypto_universe.py), and the watch/alerts subsystem in servers/orchestration.py (dedup state JSON via atomic _write_json + optional JSONL append sink _emit_file) — market alerts only, not trades. The exchange's own fill history is the only durable ledger.

extension_points.how: Monkeypatch with `lambda settings: (fake_info, fake_exchange, address)` exactly as tests/test_execution.py does; any new client method should keep all SDK access behind _ensure() so the seam stays sufficient.
extension_points.notes: Module-level function, exported in __all__; patch the module attribute (hx._build_clients), not the class.

extension_points.where: makecrazypenny/orchestration/paper_trade.py::_run and the client= kwarg on every wrapper
extension_points.how: Second injection seam: pass any object with the right method names as client=. _run is the single chokepoint every client call flows through — the natural place to add a journaling decorator that appends each call+result (already secret-scrubbed dicts) to a JSONL trade journal.
extension_points.notes: open_from_decision's return dict {symbol, interval, decision, plan, sizing, order(oids in result.statuses), account_after} is already the ideal per-trade record; persist it before returning.

extension_points.how: Add new fields (e.g. journal_dir, agent_id) as dataclass fields with env reads in from_env using the existing `os.environ.get(...) or None` / _float_env / _int_env patterns. Keep import-safe (no I/O at import).
extension_points.notes: Reuse Settings.resolve_cache_dir() for any state directory; it degrades silently on OSError.

extension_points.how: Copy these for journal persistence: atomic tmp+replace JSON writes, append-only JSONL, sha1 dedup keys. Already proven offline-safe and never-raise.

The SDK's order/market_open/market_close/bulk_orders all accept cloid (client order id) — currently never passed. Threading a deterministic cloid (e.g. hash of decision id) through open_position/open_from_decision gives durable order<->decision correlation for PnL attribution.
extension_points.notes: FakeExchange in tests already accepts cloid=None so existing tests keep passing.

extension_points.how: Add start_time/end_time params backed by info.user_fills_by_time, and a user_funding_history wrapper, to make complete realized-PnL + funding reconstruction possible for long horizons.
Register new paper_* MCP tools (e.g. paper_journal, paper_performance) following the existing pattern: thin async tool -> orchestration/paper_trade function -> _dumps.
extension_points.notes: Tool descriptions must mention key requirements and stay ASCII.

conventions: Import-safe: SDK (hyperliquid, eth_account) and dotenv imported lazily inside functions; importing any module never hits network or requires the 'trade' extra.
Testnet-locked: Settings has only hyperliquid_testnet_url; no mainnet field may be added (tests assert no attr containing 'mainnet').
Secret-safe: every error string passes through core/redact.redact_secrets; ExecutionError scrubs in __init__; never log/echo MCP_HL_PRIVATE_KEY (64-hex tokens are masked, 40-hex addresses kept).
Never-raise orchestration: paper_trade wrappers convert all exceptions to {'error': ..., 'disclaimer': DISCLAIMER} dicts; tools never crash over I/O.
Sync client + asyncio.to_thread: HyperliquidPaperClient stays synchronous (SDK uses blocking requests); async layers hop via _run.
Every user-facing payload carries core/disclaimer.DISCLAIMER; CLI output strictly ASCII.

Dependency injection over ambient env: Settings/get_api_key treats explicit None as disabled (no env fallback for known keys); tests pass Settings() directly and inject fakes via _build_clients or client= kwargs.
Offline tests: pytest with asyncio_mode=auto; no network; SDK mocked at the _build_clients seam; run with .venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest -q.

Symbols: canonical_crypto -> BASEQUOTE (BTCUSDT); to_hyperliquid_coin -> bare base (BTC). Sizes round to szDecimals; prices to 5 sig figs and <= (6 - szDecimals) decimals; $10 min notional enforced client-side.

Exchange-side protection: TP/SL are reduce-only trigger-market orders under grouping='positionTpsl' (OCO, position-tied), validated against live mid before placement; attach only after entry fill, sized to fill.
gaps: No decision journal: decide_crypto output and open_from_decision's full {decision, plan, sizing, order, account_after} record are returned to the caller and discarded — a long-running swarm has no replayable history of what was decided, why, and with what sizing.
No trade-to-decision linkage on the exchange: cloid is never set on orders, so once the tool result is gone there is no way to map a fill/oid back to the decision/thesis that produced it.

No per-trade outcome tracking: realized PnL exists only as per-fill closedPnl from recent_fills (default limit 20, no time pagination); nobody reconstructs entry->exit episodes, win/loss vs the plan's stop_price/target_price, or thesis-vs-outcome scoring.
No equity curve: account_state is point-in-time; no periodic snapshotting of account_value/tradable_usdc to disk, so drawdown/Sharpe/exposure over time cannot be computed.
Funding payments invisible: no wrapper for user_funding_history, so leveraged-perp PnL attribution (price PnL vs funding vs fees) is incomplete even though the plan estimates est_funding_cost.

No multi-agent coordination: a single wallet (MCP_HL_PRIVATE_KEY) with no agent_id/position-ownership tagging, no idempotency keys, and no locking — concurrent swarm agents would double-size against the same tradable_usdc and clobber each other's TP/SL legs (set_position_tpsl on the same coin stacks new OCO pairs; old legs must be cancelled manually per the paper_set_tpsl tool description).
No reconciliation loop: nothing compares intended state (decision journal) vs exchange state (positions/orders/fills) after restarts; recent_fills' 20-row default makes catch-up after downtime lossy.
No performance/attribution surface: no MCP tool or CLI command computes realized+unrealized PnL per trade/per strategy; would need the journal + fills pagination + funding history first.
Persistence primitives exist only in the alerts subsystem (servers/orchestration.py JSON/JSONL helpers) — they would need to be lifted into a shared module for execution-layer use.
